title: Integrate DistributedAggregationPlanner into query execution
description: Collection.aggregate() loads all documents into memory - can't scale beyond RAM. DistributedAggregationPlanner exists but not integrated. Need streaming aggregation processing in batches.
close_reason:
## Implementation Complete

### Changes Made

1. **Extended `AggregateOptions`** (`src/types.ts`):
   - Added `distributed?: boolean` option to enable distributed aggregation
   - Added `batchSize?: number` option (reserved for future streaming support)

2. **Updated `AggregationCursor`** (`src/client/aggregation.ts`):
   - Integrated `DistributedAggregationPlanner` into the cursor
   - Added `analyzePipeline()` method for pipeline analysis with caching
   - Added `canUseDistributedExecution()` to determine if pipeline benefits from distribution
   - Added `executeDistributed()` method that uses partial aggregation and merging
   - `toArray()` now checks if distributed mode should be used and delegates accordingly
   - Maintains full backwards compatibility - standard execution is default

### How It Works

When `{ distributed: true }` is passed to `aggregate()`:

1. Pipeline is analyzed to check if it can benefit from distributed execution
2. If pipeline has `$group` and no blocking stages like `$lookup`:
   - Pipeline is split into map phase (before $group), group stage, and reduce phase (after $group)
   - Map phase stages are executed first
   - `$group` uses partial aggregation with `DistributedAggregationPlanner.executePartialGroup()`
   - Partial results are merged with `mergePartialAggregates()`
   - Reduce phase stages are applied to merged results
3. If pipeline cannot benefit from distribution, falls back to standard execution

### Tests Added

- `tests/unit/client/distributed-integration.test.ts` - 18 tests (all passing)

### Limitations

- `$lookup` pipelines cannot use distributed execution (loads entire foreign collection)
- Single-node execution currently exercises the distributed code path
- Full cross-shard execution requires integration with ShardExecutor

title: Extract Iceberg to separate repository
description:
Extract Iceberg implementation to ~/projects/iceberg and dot-do/iceberg repo.

## Architecture (Three Tiers)

### Tier 1: Direct Metadata (Core)
- Read/write metadata.json, manifests, snapshots directly
- Pure functions, no external dependencies
- Works with any storage backend (R2, S3, local filesystem)
- Avro encoding for manifests

### Tier 2: R2 Data Catalog Integration (Optional)
- Integrate with Cloudflare R2 Data Catalog /iceberg REST endpoint
- Activate when CF_ICEBERG_TOKEN environment variable is set
- Fall back to Tier 1 if not configured

### Tier 3: Own Iceberg REST Server (Alternative)
- Implement Iceberg REST Catalog spec
- Use DO SQLite or D1 as metadata store (faster than file-based)
- oauth.do integration for authentication
- FGA/RBAC for fine-grained table-level permissions
- Audit logging

## Package Exports
- iceberg (core metadata)
- iceberg/catalog (R2 Data Catalog client)
- iceberg/server (REST server for Workers)
- iceberg/auth (oauth.do middleware)

## Why Extract
- Reusable for any Cloudflare Workers data lake tooling
- Independent versioning from MongoLake
- Clean separation of concerns
